evidence_items.text: Startup loads or initializes a persisted local runtime workspace under `.jeff_runtime`. On a fresh or reset runtime, Jeff initializes `project-1` / `wu-1` with no seeded run history; later startups reload the persisted canonical state and support records already present in that workspace. No daemon, GUI, or background continuation is started.

findings.text: Jeff initializes a workspace under `.jeff_runtime`, creating `project-1` / `wu-1` with no seeded run history on fresh or reset runtimes, while later startups reload persisted canonical state and support records.
The system does not start a GUI, daemon, or background continuation during startup.
Jeff is described as a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone rather than a GUI, a broad API surface, an autonomous continuation system, or a broad memo.

inferences: The system relies on local filesystem persistence to maintain state between sessions.
question: startup summary

source_items.snippet: # Jeff Jeff is a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone. It is strongest today at truthful inspection, bounded `/run`, approval-gated continuation, research support, and anti-drift coverage. It is not a GUI, a broad API surface, an autonomous continuation system, or a broad memo
source_items.title: README.md

summary: Jeff is a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone designed for truthful inspection, research support, anti-drift coverage, and approval-gated continuation. Upon startup, it initializes or loads a persisted local runtime workspace located under `.jeff_runtime`.
uncertainties: No explicit uncertainties identified from the provided evidence.
